description: The narrative layer — defines how the world is told: perspective, selection, and presentation. The narrative layer does not define its own truth. All facts live in the world layer. Narrative schemas only select, filter, and arrange world content for storytelling.

lenses.description: Narrative perspectives — filter and interpret world content (define no own truth)

formats.description: Output format definitions — purely about presentation, no content relevance

beats.description: Story beats — hierarchical narrative building blocks. Beats select world graph elements and add dramaturgical structure. Vertical chaining via parent groups beats within a storyline; horizontal chaining via threads connects beats across storylines.

devices.description: Narrative devices — rhetorical connections between beats (foreshadowing, red herrings, etc.)

threads.description: Thematic threads and motifs — horizontal chaining of beats across the storyline hierarchy. Connect beats from different storylines through shared thematic meaning.

$ref.description: Path to an external YAML file containing this item
